data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Adobe simplifies decision-making processes by reducing options where possible.
data.technical_spec: Streamlined user interfaces with clear hierarchical organization of features.

data.title: Fitts' Law
data.description: UI elements are sized and spaced for optimal speed and efficiency.
data.technical_spec: Consistent layout with frequently-used actions positioned ergonomically.

data.title: Law of Prägnanz
data.description: Designs are made to be as simple and memorable as possible, minimizing cognitive load.
data.technical_spec: Coherent visual branding and common design patterns across products.

data.title: Gestalt Principles
data.description: Visual elements are arranged to reflect a cohesive and intuitive user journey.
data.technical_spec: Groups related items together using proximity, similarity, and continuity.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Adobe aligns its interface design with user expectations based on familiar applications.
data.technical_spec: UI patterns are based on industry standards and widely adopted practices.
